content.text: I found your project files — let me dig into the Q3 roadmap, team status, risks, and recent notes to surface the most important alignment topics.

content.content:
Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Project Helios — Weekly Status Update
Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:
- Complete dashboard module v1
- Resolve data format inconsistencies (Blocker #1)
- Begin UAT preparation

Open Blockers:
1. Meridian source data contains inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs. MM/DD/YYYY).
   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.
2. Third-party mapping library (GeoSync v2.1) license renewal pending. Legal reviewing.
   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

Meeting Notes: Project Helios / Meridian Group Sync
Date: 2026-07-08
Attendees: Sarah Chen (NovaBridge PM), Derek Okafor (NovaBridge Tech Lead),
           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

Decisions:
1. Demo date confirmed: 2026-07-25 (product demo for Meridian stakeholders)
2. Meridian will provide clean sample dataset by 2026-07-15 to unblock date format issue
3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Q2 Deliverables Checklist

[DONE]      Requirements documentation v1.0
[DONE]      System architecture design
[DONE]      API gateway implementation
[DONE]      Development environment setup
[DONE]      Data schema finalization
[IN-PROGRESS] Data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
[IN-PROGRESS] Client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
[OVERDUE]   Integration test suite setup (was due 2026-06-30; rescheduled to 2026-07-20)
[OVERDUE]   UAT environment provisioning (was due 2026-07-01; blocked by Meridian IT access)
[OVERDUE]   Performance benchmarking report (was due 2026-07-05; owner: Derek Okafor)

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14
R-03  Dashboard performance on large datasets       Medium      High    6      Derek Okafor        Active — benchmark task HEL-247 not yet started
R-04  UAT timeline slippage                        High        Medium  6      Sarah Chen          Active — 1-week extension already consumed
R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Team Roster

Name: Sarah Chen        | Title: Project Manager       | Email: s.chen@novabridge.com
Name: Derek Okafor      | Title: Technical Lead         | Email: d.okafor@novabridge.com
Name: Amir Hassan       | Title: Senior Backend Engineer| Email: a.hassan@novabridge.com
Name: Liu Wei           | Title: Backend Engineer       | Email: l.wei@novabridge.com
Name: Fatima Al-Rashid  | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: f.alrashid@novabridge.com
Name: Tomas Rivera      | Title: Frontend Engineer      | Email: t.rivera@novabridge.com
Name: Kenji Watanabe    | Title: QA Engineer            | Email: k.watanabe@novabridge.com
Name: Nadia Petrov      | Title: DevOps Engineer        | Email: n.petrov@novabridge.com
Name: Marcus Green      | Title: Data Engineer          | Email: m.green@novabridge.com
Name: Isabelle Dupont   | Title: UX Designer            | Email: i.dupont@novabridge.com
Name: Raj Patel         | Title: Business Analyst       | Email: r.patel@novabridge.com
Name: Olivia Brooks     | Title: Solutions Architect    | Email: o.brooks@novabridge.com

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

Sprint 14 Task Assignments (2026-07-07 to 2026-07-18)

Task ID  | Description                               | Assignee          | Points | Due Date   | Status
---------|-------------------------------------------|-------------------|--------|------------|------------
HEL-241  | Dashboard: data table component           | Fatima Al-Rashid  | 5      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress
HEL-242  | Dashboard: chart rendering module         | Tomas Rivera      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | In Progress
HEL-243  | Data ingestion: date format normalization | Amir Hassan       | 5      | 2026-07-15 | Blocked
HEL-244  | Integration test suite: auth flows        | Kenji Watanabe    | 3      | 2026-07-14 | Done
HEL-245  | Integration test suite: data pipeline     | Kenji Watanabe    | 5      | 2026-07-18 | In Progress
HEL-246  | UAT environment: server provisioning      | Nadia Petrov      | 8      | 2026-07-16 | At Risk
HEL-247  | Performance benchmark: dashboard load     | Derek Okafor      | 3      | 2026-07-18 | Not Started
HEL-248  | GeoSync license renewal coordination      | Raj Patel         | 2      | 2026-07-14 | In Progress

content.text: I've got a clear picture of Q3 priorities, blockers, and risks. Now I'll build your 90-minute kickoff agenda and save it.

input.content:
Project Helios — Q3 Kickoff Meeting Agenda
Duration: 90 minutes
Date: Week of July 14, 2026 (TBD - next week)
Facilitator: Sarah Chen (Project Manager)
Attendees: Project Helios Core Team - Sarah Chen, Derek Okafor, Amir Hassan, Liu Wei, Fatima Al-Rashid, Tomas Rivera, Kenji Watanabe, Nadia Petrov, Marcus Green, Isabelle Dupont, Raj Patel, Olivia Brooks

Objective: Align the team on Q3 priorities, critical July milestones, and launch readiness for Oct 1 target.

Pre-Read (please review before meeting):
- q3_roadmap_draft.txt
- project_helios_status.txt (Week of 2026-07-06)
- risk_register.txt
- meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt (Meridian sync)

---

AGENDA

1. Welcome & Objectives (0:00 - 0:05) - 5 min
   Facilitator: Sarah Chen
   - Meeting purpose and outcomes
   - Q3 theme: Integration → Demo → UAT → Launch

2. Q2 Recap & Q3 Starting Line (0:05 - 0:15) - 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Q2 Wins: Requirements doc, architecture, API gateway, dev env, data schema - all DONE
   - Overdue carryover: Integration test suite setup (rescheduled to 7/20), UAT env provisioning (blocked by Meridian IT), Performance benchmarking report (owner: Derek Okafor)
   - Current status: ON TRACK (minor risks) - Data ingestion 85%, Dashboard 60%, Integration testing 40%

3. Q3 Roadmap Overview (0:15 - 0:30) - 15 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor
   - Target launch: Oct 1, 2026 — Risk: UAT slippage >2 weeks triggers penalty review per contract Section 7.1
   - Five pillars:
     a) Complete System Integration (July)
     b) Product Demo for Meridian - July 25
     c) UAT with Meridian Group - Aug 17-28 (extended by 1 week)
     d) Performance Hardening - Aug-Sep (Dashboard <2 sec for 100k records, 500k records/min ingestion)
     e) Launch Prep - September (prod env, runbook, hypercare, training)
   - Proposed Q3 additions (pending change control): Scheduled ingestion jobs, email alerting for failures, self-service mapping editor

4. July Critical Path - Integration & Demo Readiness (0:30 - 0:50) - 20 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor (Tech Lead) + Leads
   - Milestones this month:
     * Data ingestion module finalize: 7/18 (HEL-243 - currently Blocked - date format normalization)
     * Client dashboard v1: 7/22 (HEL-241, HEL-242 in progress)
     * Integration test sign-off: 7/25 (HEL-244 done, HEL-245 in progress)
   - Demo Requirements (per Meridian 7/08 sync & Contract Sec 9.3):
     * Must include LIVE data ingestion with Meridian sample data (not mock)
     * End-to-end pipeline walkthrough + dashboard with real data
     * Meridian clean sample dataset due 7/15 to unblock blocker R-01
     * IT infra diagram due from Meridian 7/12, UAT access provisioned by 7/19
   - Blockers to resolve before demo:
     * R-01: Inconsistent date formats (ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY) - Owner: Amir Hassan - awaiting Meridian dataset
     * R-02: GeoSync v2.1 license renewal - Owner: Raj Patel / Derek Okafor - due 7/14, legal review active
   - Tasks at risk: HEL-246 UAT server provisioning (Nadia Petrov), HEL-247 Performance benchmark (Derek Okafor - not yet started)
   - Alignment needed: Benchmark dashboard load times before demo (concern raised by Meridian)

5. UAT Planning (0:50 - 1:05) - 15 min
   Owner: Kenji Watanabe (QA) / Nadia Petrov (DevOps)
   - UAT window: Aug 17-28
   - Environment provisioning plan and dependencies on Meridian IT
   - Test coverage: auth flows, data pipeline, dashboard scenarios
   - Support model for Meridian user testing sessions
   - Budget for UAT feedback: 2 sprints

6. Performance, Security & Launch Preparation (1:05 - 1:15) - 10 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor / Nadia Petrov
   - Performance targets: <2s dashboard load, 500k records/min sustained
   - Security pen test vendor selection - TBD
   - Prod env setup, runbook documentation, 30-day hypercare plan, client training
   - Architecture reminder: stateless nodes, TLS 1.3 / AES-256, GDPR PII handling, multi-tenant isolation

7. Top Risks & Mitigations (1:15 - 1:25) - 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Walk risk register:
     R-01 Data quality (High/High) - Mitigation: Meridian clean dataset 7/15, normalization logic in HEL-243
     R-02 GeoSync license (Med/High) - Mitigation: Legal expedite, contingency check
     R-03 Dashboard perf on large datasets (Med/High) - Mitigation: Start HEL-247 immediately
     R-04 UAT slippage (High/Med) - 1-week extension already consumed
     R-06 Meridian IT access delays (Med/Med) - Follow up on infra diagram
     R-05 Key person dependency: Derek Okafor - Plan backup
     R-07 Scope creep - 3 informal feature requests logged
   - Open discussion: What else could derail Oct 1?

8. Roles, Ownership & Next Steps (1:25 - 1:30) - 5 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Confirm Sprint 14 ownership (HEL-241 to HEL-248)
   - Action items to close pre-demo:
     * Raj Patel/Derek: GeoSync renewal by 7/14
     * Amir Hassan: Date normalization unblocked by 7/15 dataset
     * Nadia Petrov: UAT env risk mitigation plan
     * Derek Okafor: Start dashboard benchmark HEL-247
     * Sarah Chen: Draft demo agenda to Meridian by 7/18
   - Communication cadence for Q3
   - Q&A and parking lot

---

Outcomes Expected:
- Clear ownership for July 18/22/25 milestones
- Agreement on demo scope (live data) and unblock plan
- Alignment on UAT readiness responsibilities
- Risk mitigation owners confirmed

Next Meeting: Sprint 14 Review / Demo Prep Check - Week of July 21

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Overall sprint 14 test status: 10/15 test cases executed, 2 FAIL (open), 3 not yet run.

[NovaBridge Team]

Project Helios Team — Mid-Year Performance Reviews 2026

Review period: January–June 2026

---
Amir Hassan — Senior Backend Engineer
Rating: Exceeds Expectations
Comments: Delivered data ingestion architecture ahead of schedule. Strong systems thinker
with excellent cross-team communication. Recommend for Senior II promotion consideration in H2.

---
Liu Wei — Backend Engineer

practices — sprint notes are often incomplete. No performance concerns.

---
Fatima Al-Rashid — Frontend Engineer

Comments: Dashboard work is on track. Occasional miscommunication with UX team noted;
suggest pairing sessions with Isabelle Dupont to improve alignment.

---
Tomas Rivera — Frontend Engineer

Comments: Two missed sprint commitments in Q2 (HEL-198, HEL-217). Performance improvement
plan initiated 2026-06-15. Specific targets set for sprint velocity and communication.
Re-evaluation scheduled for 2026-09-01.

---

Comments: QA coverage is excellent. Proactively flagged two critical defects before client
demo risk materialized. Recommend for QA Lead role in next budget cycle.

---
Marcus Green — Data Engineer
Rating: Meets Expectations
Comments: Strong on data pipeline work. Slower to adapt to Spark framework than expected;
Derek providing direct mentorship. On track to meet standards by Q3.

NovaBridge Solutions — Office Lease Renewal Notes

Property: 1240 Innovation Drive, Suite 400, Austin TX 78701

Landlord: Harwick Commercial Properties LLC

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between
NovaBridge Solutions Inc. ("Disclosing Party") and [VENDOR NAME] ("Receiving Party").

1. Confidential Information
The Receiving Party agrees to hold in strict confidence all technical, business,
and operational information disclosed by the Disclosing Party, including but not
limited to source code, system architecture, client data, and business processes.

2. Term
This Agreement shall remain in effect for three (3) years from the date of signature,
unless terminated earlier by written agreement of both parties.

3. Permitted Disclosures
The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to employees with a
demonstrated need to know, provided such employees are bound by equivalent obligations.

4. Return of Materials
Upon request or termination, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or certify
destruction of all Confidential Information and copies thereof.

5. Governing Law
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties execute this Agreement as of the date below.

NovaBridge Solutions Inc.

Project Helios — Q3 Kickoff Meeting Agenda
Duration: 90 minutes
Date: Week of July 14, 2026 (Next Week)
Facilitator: Sarah Chen (Project Manager)
Attendees: Helios Core Team - Sarah Chen, Derek Okafor, Amir Hassan, Liu Wei, Fatima Al-Rashid, Tomas Rivera, Kenji Watanabe, Nadia Petrov, Marcus Green, Isabelle Dupont, Raj Patel, Olivia Brooks

Objective: Align on Q3 priorities, critical July milestones, and launch readiness for Oct 1 target.

Pre-Reads (please review beforehand):
- q3_roadmap_draft.txt
- project_helios_status.txt (Week of 2026-07-06)
- risk_register.txt
- meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt (Meridian Sync)
- helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt (QA Status)

------------------------------------------------------------
AGENDA BREAKDOWN

1. Welcome & Objectives (0:00-0:05) — 5 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Meeting purpose: Set Q3 alignment
   - Q3 Theme: Integration → Demo (July 25) → UAT (Aug 17-28) → Launch Prep → Oct 1 Launch

2. Q2 Recap & Current State (0:05-0:15) — 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - DONE in Q2: Requirements doc v1.0, architecture design, API gateway, dev env, data schema
   - IN-PROGRESS: Data ingestion 85%, Client dashboard 60%, Integration testing 40%
   - OVERDUE → Rescheduled: Integration test suite setup (now 7/20), UAT env provisioning (blocked by Meridian IT), Perf benchmarking report (owner Derek Okafor)
   - Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

3. Q3 Roadmap Overview (0:15-0:30) — 15 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor
   - Target Launch: 2026-10-01 — Note: UAT slippage >2 weeks risks Section 7.1 penalty review
   - 5 Pillars:
     a) System Integration Complete (July) - targets 7/18 ingestion, 7/22 dashboard, 7/25 sign-off
     b) Product Demo for Meridian - July 25 - must be LIVE data per Meridian request & Contract Sec 9.3
     c) UAT - Meridian Group Aug 17-28 (extended by 1 week already)
     d) Performance Hardening Aug-Sep - targets: <2 sec dashboard for 100k records, 500k records/min ingestion sustained
     e) Launch Prep Sep - prod env, runbook, 30-day hypercare, final training
   - Proposed additions pending change control: Cron-based scheduled jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

4. July Critical Path: Integration & Demo Readiness (0:30-0:50) — 20 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor + Amir, Fatima, Tomas, Kenji, Nadia, Raj
   - Sprint 14 Task Review:
     HEL-241 Dashboard data table (Fatima) - In Progress - Due 7/14
     HEL-242 Dashboard chart rendering (Tomas) - In Progress - Due 7/16
     HEL-243 Date format normalization (Amir) - BLOCKED - Due 7/15
     HEL-244 Auth flows integration tests (Kenji) - DONE
     HEL-245 Data pipeline integration tests (Kenji) - In Progress - Due 7/18
     HEL-246 UAT env server provisioning (Nadia) - At Risk - Due 7/16
     HEL-247 Perf benchmark dashboard load (Derek) - NOT STARTED - Due 7/18
     HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal (Raj) - In Progress - Due 7/14
   - QA Testing Log Sprint 14 Update (Kenji):
     * Auth Flows: 4/4 PASS
     * Data Pipeline Batch: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL - Both HIGH severity, owned by Amir Hassan
       - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format - FAIL TC-505 - Affects ~40% Meridian source data. Blocked pending Meridian clean sample (due 7/15)
       - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion >200k records causes memory overflow at ~210k - FAIL TC-507 - Root cause under investigation
     * Dashboard Suite: 1/4 complete - TC-601 PASS, TC-602 In Progress, TC-603/604 NOT RUN
     * Overall: 10/15 cases executed
   - Demo Requirements confirmed 7/08:
     * LIVE data ingestion using Meridian sample data (no mocks) - emphasized by James Whitfield
     * End-to-end pipeline walkthrough + dashboard with real data
     * Dependencies: Meridian clean dataset by 7/15 (Priya Kapoor), IT infra diagram by 7/12, UAT access by 7/19
   - Key Blockers for Demo:
     R-01 Data quality / date formats - Owner: Amir - awaiting dataset
     R-02 GeoSync v2.1 license renewal - Owner: Raj/Legal - due 7/14 before demo

5. UAT Planning (0:50-1:05) — 15 min
   Owner: Kenji Watanabe / Nadia Petrov
   - UAT Window: Aug 17-28, deploy to UAT env, support Meridian testing sessions
   - Environment readiness: HEL-246 at risk, needs Meridian IT diagram + access
   - Test coverage plan: auth, data pipeline (including fixes for HEL-D-014/015), dashboard load benchmarks
   - Budget: 2 sprints allocated for UAT feedback fixes

6. Performance, Security & Launch Prep (1:05-1:15) — 10 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor / Nadia Petrov
   - Perf targets: Dashboard <2s for 100k, ingestion 500k/min - HEL-D-015 overflow shows we are not there yet
   - Tasks to start ASAP: HEL-247 dashboard load benchmark (TC-604)
   - Security: External pen test vendor TBD
   - Prod readiness: Env setup, runbook docs, hypercare 30-day model, client training
   - Architecture guardrails: Stateless nodes, TLS 1.3 / AES-256, GDPR data minimization, multi-tenant isolation via schema

7. Top Risks & Mitigations (1:15-1:25) — 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Risk Register Review (as of 7/07):
     R-01 Source data quality - High/High/9 - Active - linked to HEL-D-014
     R-02 GeoSync license - Med/High/6 - Active - due 7/14
     R-03 Dashboard perf large datasets - Med/High/6 - Active - HEL-247 not started + TC-604 not run
     R-04 UAT slippage - High/Med/6 - Active - 1-week extension already consumed
     R-05 Key person dependency Derek Okafor - Low/High/3 - Monitor - no backup tech lead
     R-06 Meridian IT access delays - Med/Med/4 - Active
     R-07 Scope creep - Low/Med/2 - Monitor - 3 informal Meridian requests logged
     + NEW from QA: Large file memory overflow (HEL-D-015) - should be added to register
   - Discussion: What else threatens Oct 1?

8. Roles, Ownership & Next Steps (1:25-1:30) — 5 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Immediate actions before demo:
     * Raj Patel/Derek Okafor: Close GeoSync renewal by 7/14
     * Amir Hassan: Fix HEL-D-014 & HEL-D-015 - unblocked 7/15, deep dive on memory issue
     * Nadia Petrov: Mitigation plan for UAT provisioning risk
     * Derek Okafor: Start HEL-247 benchmark + support HEL-D-015 RCA
     * Kenji Watanabe: Complete dashboard tests TC-602/603/604
     * Sarah Chen: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 7/18
   - Confirm communication cadence for Q3
   - Parking lot & Close

------------------------------------------------------------
Expected Outcomes:
- Clear owners for July 18/22/25 milestones
- Agreement on live-data demo scope and unblock plan for HEL-D-014/015
- UAT readiness plan with dependencies
- Risk mitigation owners re-confirmed

Next Meeting: Sprint 14 Review / Demo Prep Check - Week of July 21

content.content:
Project Helios — Q3 Kickoff Meeting Agenda
Duration: 90 minutes
Date: Week of July 14, 2026 (Next Week)
Facilitator: Sarah Chen (Project Manager)
Attendees: Helios Core Team - Sarah Chen, Derek Okafor, Amir Hassan, Liu Wei, Fatima Al-Rashid, Tomas Rivera, Kenji Watanabe, Nadia Petrov, Marcus Green, Isabelle Dupont, Raj Patel, Olivia Brooks

Objective: Align on Q3 priorities, critical July milestones, and launch readiness for Oct 1 target.

Pre-Reads (please review beforehand):
- q3_roadmap_draft.txt
- project_helios_status.txt (Week of 2026-07-06)
- risk_register.txt
- meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt (Meridian Sync)
- helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt (QA Status)

------------------------------------------------------------
AGENDA BREAKDOWN

1. Welcome & Objectives (0:00-0:05) — 5 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Meeting purpose: Set Q3 alignment
   - Q3 Theme: Integration → Demo (July 25) → UAT (Aug 17-28) → Launch Prep → Oct 1 Launch

2. Q2 Recap & Current State (0:05-0:15) — 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - DONE in Q2: Requirements doc v1.0, architecture design, API gateway, dev env, data schema
   - IN-PROGRESS: Data ingestion 85%, Client dashboard 60%, Integration testing 40%
   - OVERDUE → Rescheduled: Integration test suite setup (now 7/20), UAT env provisioning (blocked by Meridian IT), Perf benchmarking report (owner Derek Okafor)
   - Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

3. Q3 Roadmap Overview (0:15-0:30) — 15 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor
   - Target Launch: 2026-10-01 — Note: UAT slippage >2 weeks risks Section 7.1 penalty review
   - 5 Pillars:
     a) System Integration Complete (July) - targets 7/18 ingestion, 7/22 dashboard, 7/25 sign-off
     b) Product Demo for Meridian - July 25 - must be LIVE data per Meridian request & Contract Sec 9.3
     c) UAT - Meridian Group Aug 17-28 (extended by 1 week already)
     d) Performance Hardening Aug-Sep - targets: <2 sec dashboard for 100k records, 500k records/min ingestion sustained
     e) Launch Prep Sep - prod env, runbook, 30-day hypercare, final training
   - Proposed additions pending change control: Cron-based scheduled jobs, email alerting for pipeline failures, self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

4. July Critical Path: Integration & Demo Readiness (0:30-0:50) — 20 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor + Amir, Fatima, Tomas, Kenji, Nadia, Raj
   - Sprint 14 Task Review:
     HEL-241 Dashboard data table (Fatima) - In Progress - Due 7/14
     HEL-242 Dashboard chart rendering (Tomas) - In Progress - Due 7/16
     HEL-243 Date format normalization (Amir) - BLOCKED - Due 7/15
     HEL-244 Auth flows integration tests (Kenji) - DONE
     HEL-245 Data pipeline integration tests (Kenji) - In Progress - Due 7/18
     HEL-246 UAT env server provisioning (Nadia) - At Risk - Due 7/16
     HEL-247 Perf benchmark dashboard load (Derek) - NOT STARTED - Due 7/18
     HEL-248 GeoSync license renewal (Raj) - In Progress - Due 7/14
   - QA Testing Log Sprint 14 Update (Kenji):
     * Auth Flows: 4/4 PASS
     * Data Pipeline Batch: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL - Both HIGH severity, owned by Amir Hassan
       - HEL-D-014: Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY format - FAIL TC-505 - Affects ~40% Meridian source data. Blocked pending Meridian clean sample (due 7/15)
       - HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion >200k records causes memory overflow at ~210k - FAIL TC-507 - Root cause under investigation
     * Dashboard Suite: 1/4 complete - TC-601 PASS, TC-602 In Progress, TC-603/604 NOT RUN
     * Overall: 10/15 cases executed
   - Demo Requirements confirmed 7/08:
     * LIVE data ingestion using Meridian sample data (no mocks) - emphasized by James Whitfield
     * End-to-end pipeline walkthrough + dashboard with real data
     * Dependencies: Meridian clean dataset by 7/15 (Priya Kapoor), IT infra diagram by 7/12, UAT access by 7/19
   - Key Blockers for Demo:
     R-01 Data quality / date formats - Owner: Amir - awaiting dataset
     R-02 GeoSync v2.1 license renewal - Owner: Raj/Legal - due 7/14 before demo

5. UAT Planning (0:50-1:05) — 15 min
   Owner: Kenji Watanabe / Nadia Petrov
   - UAT Window: Aug 17-28, deploy to UAT env, support Meridian testing sessions
   - Environment readiness: HEL-246 at risk, needs Meridian IT diagram + access
   - Test coverage plan: auth, data pipeline (including fixes for HEL-D-014/015), dashboard load benchmarks
   - Budget: 2 sprints allocated for UAT feedback fixes

6. Performance, Security & Launch Prep (1:05-1:15) — 10 min
   Owner: Derek Okafor / Nadia Petrov
   - Perf targets: Dashboard <2s for 100k, ingestion 500k/min - HEL-D-015 overflow shows we are not there yet
   - Tasks to start ASAP: HEL-247 dashboard load benchmark (TC-604)
   - Security: External pen test vendor TBD
   - Prod readiness: Env setup, runbook docs, hypercare 30-day model, client training
   - Architecture guardrails: Stateless nodes, TLS 1.3 / AES-256, GDPR data minimization, multi-tenant isolation via schema

7. Top Risks & Mitigations (1:15-1:25) — 10 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Risk Register Review (as of 7/07):
     R-01 Source data quality - High/High/9 - Active - linked to HEL-D-014
     R-02 GeoSync license - Med/High/6 - Active - due 7/14
     R-03 Dashboard perf large datasets - Med/High/6 - Active - HEL-247 not started + TC-604 not run
     R-04 UAT slippage - High/Med/6 - Active - 1-week extension already consumed
     R-05 Key person dependency Derek Okafor - Low/High/3 - Monitor - no backup tech lead
     R-06 Meridian IT access delays - Med/Med/4 - Active
     R-07 Scope creep - Low/Med/2 - Monitor - 3 informal Meridian requests logged
     + NEW from QA: Large file memory overflow (HEL-D-015) - should be added to register
   - Discussion: What else threatens Oct 1?

8. Roles, Ownership & Next Steps (1:25-1:30) — 5 min
   Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Immediate actions before demo:
     * Raj Patel/Derek Okafor: Close GeoSync renewal by 7/14
     * Amir Hassan: Fix HEL-D-014 & HEL-D-015 - unblocked 7/15, deep dive on memory issue
     * Nadia Petrov: Mitigation plan for UAT provisioning risk
     * Derek Okafor: Start HEL-247 benchmark + support HEL-D-015 RCA
     * Kenji Watanabe: Complete dashboard tests TC-602/603/604
     * Sarah Chen: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 7/18
   - Confirm communication cadence for Q3
   - Parking lot & Close

------------------------------------------------------------
Expected Outcomes:
- Clear owners for July 18/22/25 milestones
- Agreement on live-data demo scope and unblock plan for HEL-D-014/015
- UAT readiness plan with dependencies
- Risk mitigation owners re-confirmed

Next Meeting: Sprint 14 Review / Demo Prep Check - Week of July 21

content.text:
Agenda created and saved as **q3_kickoff_agenda.txt**.

It's a 90-minute structured agenda for next week (week of July 14) built from your actual project files:

**Structure:**
1. **Welcome** (5 min) - Sarah Chen
2. **Q2 Recap & Current State** (10 min) - Covers DONE items, ON TRACK status with 85% ingestion / 60% dashboard, and 3 overdue carryovers
3. **Q3 Roadmap** (15 min) - Oct 1 launch target, 5 pillars (Integration → July 25 Demo → Aug 17-28 UAT → Perf Hardening → Launch Prep), plus proposed additions pending change control
4. **July Critical Path** (20 min) - Deep dive on Sprint 14 tasks HEL-241 to HEL-248, QA failures HEL-D-014 (date format affects 40% data) & HEL-D-015 (memory overflow >200k), and live-data demo requirements per Meridian sync
5. **UAT Planning** (15 min) - Environment, coverage, 2-sprint budget
6. **Perf, Security & Launch Prep** (10 min) - <2s dashboard, 500k/min ingestion, pen test TBD
7. **Top Risks** (10 min) - R-01 to R-07 + new HEL-D-015 overflow
8. **Roles & Next Steps** (5 min) - Immediate pre-demo actions with owners and due dates

Pre-reads listed and all blockers/dependencies (Meridian clean dataset 7/15, GeoSync license 7/14, infra diagram 7/12) are called out to align the team before Q3.
